That file
dd48de7dc9048583.ai_summary_short:
**Root Cause:** The job failed during the `telco5g-ptp-cluster-setup` phase, before any PTP tests ran. The Ansible playbook on the kcli host (`10.8.34.218`) deployed cluster `ptpcimno` via `kcli create plan ... -P tag=4.12 -P version=nightly`, then polled for `/root/cluster_ready.txt` on the installer VM. That file never appeared after 150 retries (~2h38m). `openshift-install` reported: *"Cluster initialization failed because one or more operators are not functioning properly"* with `MultipleErrors`. `ClusterVersion` remained stuck for 144+ minutes at `Unable to apply 4.12.0-0.nightly-2026-06-05-032308: an unknown error has occurred: MultipleErrors`, with all cluster operators unavailable. The API server (`10.8.34.117:6443`) eventually became unreachable (`connection refused`). BareMetalHosts were online, but the control plane never became fully functional. Downstream gather/must-gather steps failed as a consequence.

**Breaking PR/Commit:** Not identifiable from the ptp-operator repo. This is `periodic-ci-openshift-release-main-nightly-4.12-e2e-telco5g-ptp` from `openshift/release` (revision `main`), running on telco5g bare-metal lab infrastructure. Recent ptp-operator commits (ART image sync, netdevsim CI, podman 5.x) are unrelated to OCP cluster bootstrap. No ptp-operator change introduced this regression.

**Affected Images:** OpenShift release payload `4.12.0-0.nightly-2026-06-05-032308` served from the lab registry at `10-8-34-216.sslip.io:5000/openshift/release-images@sha256:9b20418e019c81adf3e553b37405d84a6ee1c62f90902a355195a454df7bbaef`. PTP operator / linuxptp-daemon images were not exercised — the job never reached PTP conformance tests.

**Is it a flake?** yes — Recent history is mixed. The May 30 run (`2060511952139980800`) reached PTP conformance tests and failed there; the April 18 run also reached PTP tests. The May 15 run (`2055438526215884800`) and this June 5 run both failed at cluster setup with the same `MultipleErrors` / `Installation not fini

ba2e3f4453f86d85.root_cause: Four BC clock-class e2e tests fail because boundary-clock test configs are created with `gmCapable 0`. Without `gmCapable 1`, a T-BC does not degrade its clock class (e.g. to freerun class 248) when upstream sync is lost — so the outage-recovery test times out waiting for class to move off Locked (6
ba2e3f4453f86d85.ai_summary_short:
**Root Cause:** Four BC clock-class e2e tests fail because boundary-clock test configs are created with `gmCapable 0`. Without `gmCapable 1`, a T-BC does not degrade its clock class (e.g. to freerun class 248) when upstream sync is lost — so the outage-recovery test times out waiting for class to move off Locked (6). The clockClass verification tests call `EnableGMCapableInPlace()` to flip `gmCapable 0 → 1`, then poll `/var/run/ptp4l.0.config` for the literal string `"gmCapable 1"`. That never appears within 120s on this job because (a) the in-place PtpConfig edit must propagate operator → `ptp-configmap` → linuxptp-daemon hot-reload, and (b) the test only checked the pod config file, not the configmap reconcile step. The job runs **main-branch tests** (`TEST_BRANCH=main`) against **release-4.13 images** (`PTP_UNDER_TEST_BRANCH=release-4.13`; only `ptp-operator:4.13` is rebuilt in CI, daemon/sidecar use pre-built 4.13 images), compounding propagation timing on an older stack. PR #215 (`6d2f1dd`) added NetworkManager suppression to `TurnOffAndWaitFaulty` but not to `TurnAllPortsDown`, which the outage test uses.

**Breaking PR/Commit:** Tests introduced by commit `0096e6b` / PR #197 (clockClass verify) and `da824bf` (outage recovery). Not an operator regression — a test/config mismatch exposed when main-branch tests run on the OCP 4.13 telco5g matrix.

**Affected Images:**
- `image-registry.../openshift-ptp/ptp-operator:4.13` (built from `release-4.13` in CI)
- `image-registry.../openshift-ptp/linuxptp-daemon:4.13` (pre-built, not rebuilt for downstream 4.13 runs)
- `image-registry.../openshift-ptp/cloud-event-proxy:4.13` (pre-built)

**Is it a flake?** no — All four failures are deterministic timeouts on the same assertions (`gmCapable 1` missing from config; clock class stuck at 6). Root cause is incorrect test configuration and incomplete NM/config propagation handling, not transient infra.

**Issue Class:** test_failure

**Suggested Fix:** Applied a test-side fix

fa2536d0f721ff9b.root_cause: On OCP 4.14, three BC clock-class e2e tests failed because the tests read the node-level `openshift_ptp_clock_class` Prometheus metric (`{node,process}` without a `config` label), which does not update when a Boundary Clock changes clock class. PMC confirms ptp4l behavior is correct: after bringing
fa2536d0f721ff9b.ai_summary_short:
**Root Cause:** On OCP 4.14, three BC clock-class e2e tests failed because the tests read the node-level `openshift_ptp_clock_class` Prometheus metric (`{node,process}` without a `config` label), which does not update when a Boundary Clock changes clock class. PMC confirms ptp4l behavior is correct: after bringing slave interface `ens2f3` down, PMC immediately reports clock class **248** while the metric stays at **6** for the full 300s timeout. This is a test/metrics mismatch, not a PTP sync failure — 61 other tests passed including offset/sync checks. The linuxptp-daemon BC event path stores clock-class changes internally but does not refresh the legacy node-level metric on OCP < 4.18; per-config metrics (added in 4.18) are the reliable source on newer releases.

**Breaking PR/Commit:** [PR #210](https://github.com/k8snetworkplumbingwg/ptp-operator/pull/210) / commit `8b697a3` (CNF-22878, merged Apr 15 2026) — added node-level metric verification for OCP < 4.18 single-NIC BC in `VerifyNICClockClass`. The outage recovery test (`da824bf`, Mar 2026) also relies on metrics-only checks via `checkClockClassStateReturnBool`, which have the same stale-metric problem on 4.14.

**Affected Images:** `linuxptp-daemon:4.14` (metrics exporter behavior), `ptp-operator:4.14`, `cloud-event-proxy:4.14`. Tests run from `main` branch conformance code against `release-4.14` operator images on OCP 4.14.0 (`PTP operator version from RELEASE_VERSION: 4.14.0`).

**Is it a flake?** no — Deterministic and reproducible. Junit logs show PMC=248 vs metric=6 on the first poll after link down; the metric never changes across 60+ polls. Dual-NIC clockClass verification passed because it already skips metrics on OCP < 4.18 (PMC only).

**Issue Class:** test_regression

**Suggested Fix:** On OCP < 4.18, verify BC clock class via PMC only (skip stale node-level Prometheus metrics). On OCP >= 4.18, keep per-config metric checks. Applied fix:
1. `VerifyNICClockClass` — removed node-level metric assert

922b3ccbfe80cd29.root_cause: The `periodic-ci-openshift-release-main-nightly-4.15-e2e-telco5g-ptp` job failed because three Boundary Clock (BC) serial clock-class e2e tests timed out (~348–382s, matching 5-minute `Eventually` windows). All failures are in the BC clock-class test suite running on OCP 4.15 (`PTP_UNDER_TEST_BRANCH
922b3ccbfe80cd29.ai_summary_short:
**Root Cause:** The `periodic-ci-openshift-release-main-nightly-4.15-e2e-telco5g-ptp` job failed because three Boundary Clock (BC) serial clock-class e2e tests timed out (~348–382s, matching 5-minute `Eventually` windows). All failures are in the BC clock-class test suite running on OCP 4.15 (`PTP_UNDER_TEST_BRANCH=release-4.15`, conformance tests from `TEST_BRANCH=main`):

1. **`[bc-serial] Verify clockClass when locking PTP source on single NIC boundary clock`** (348s error) — After bringing the slave interface down, the test expects clockClass 248 (freerun) via PMC and the node-level `openshift_ptp_clock_class` metric. On OCP 4.15 the node-level metric (no `config` label) does not reliably track freerun transitions, while dual-NIC runs skip metrics and pass via PMC only. The test hung waiting for metrics to reach 248.

2. **`[bc-serial] BC clock class recovers to Locked after upstream link outage`** (382s error) — Two compounding issues:
   - `TurnAllPortsDown`/`TurnAllPortsUp` did not disable NetworkManager, so RHCOS NM auto-recovered interfaces within seconds (the same race fixed for `TurnOffAndWaitFaulty` in commit `6d2f1dd`/PR #215, but not applied to the bulk port-down path).
   - BC test configs ship with `gmCapable 0`; without `gmCapable 1`, ptp4l does not change clockClass on sync loss, so the test never saw degradation away from Locked (6).

3. **`[dualnicbc-serial]` suite — 1 error** (evidence truncated at 20KB; suite summary shows 1 error) — Same `BC clock class recovers to Locked after upstream link outage` test running in dual-NIC BC mode, failing for the same NM + `gmCapable 0` reasons.

Core PTP sync tests passed (slave sync, downstream BC sync, dual-NIC clockClass locking). This is a test-harness/compatibility issue, not operator image corruption or cluster infra failure.

**Breaking PR/Commit:** Not a single operator regression. Failures stem from test additions and incomplete hardening:
- `da824bf` / PR #185 — added the T-BC outage clock-class r

7f2e9aff26f320c2.root_cause: The telco5g-ptp test never got a running PTP operator, so the `linuxptp-daemon` DaemonSet was never created. The chain is:
7f2e9aff26f320c2.ai_summary_short:
**Root Cause:** The telco5g-ptp test never got a running PTP operator, so the `linuxptp-daemon` DaemonSet was never created. The chain is:

1. **Operator image build failed** in the in-cluster podman pod:
   ```
   FROM registry.ci.openshift.org/ocp/builder:rhel-9-golang-1.22-openshift-4.18
   Error: ... manifest unknown
   make: *** [Makefile:122: docker-build] Error 125
   ```

2. **The CI script masked the failure.** The podman build script runs `set -xe`, then `set +xe` to hide registry credentials during login, but never restores `-e`. Build and push errors were ignored and the script printed `[INFO] index build succeeded`.

3. **`make deploy` ran anyway** with only `IMG=...` (no `LINUXPTP_DAEMON_IMAGE` / `SIDECAR_EVENT_IMAGE`). It created CRDs, RBAC, and the `ptp-operator` Deployment, but the operator image was never pushed to the cluster registry.

4. **Without a running operator**, the `PtpOperatorConfig` reconciler never ran `syncLinuxptpDaemon()`, so the `linuxptp-daemon` DaemonSet was never created.

5. The test then polled `kubectl rollout status daemonset linuxptp-daemon -n openshift-ptp` for ~400 seconds and got `NotFound` every time.

Gathered cluster state (`gather-extra__pods.json`, `gather-extra__config-resources.json`) shows no `openshift-ptp` workloads, consistent with a failed deployment.

**Breaking PR/Commit:** Not identifiable to a specific ptp-operator commit. The immediate trigger is the missing OCP builder image `registry.ci.openshift.org/ocp/builder:rhel-9-golang-1.22-openshift-4.18` referenced by `Dockerfile` on the `release-4.18` branch. That is likely an ART/ocp-build-data registry change rather than a recent ptp-operator code regression. The CI script’s missing `set -e` after credential handling is a separate bug in the openshift/release telco5g test harness.

**Affected Images:**
- `image-registry.openshift-image-registry.svc:5000/openshift-ptp/ptp-operator:4.18` — build failed, never pushed
- `image-registry.openshift-image-registry

a8893477c251bcee.root_cause: Four PTP boundary-clock clockClass tests failed in telco5g-ptp CI (bc, dualnicbc, dualnicbcha serial suites). Three failures are confirmed from test_results.json; all are clockClass-related timeouts (~379–385s, consistent with 5-minute Ginkgo Eventually windows). The "BC clock class recovers to Lock
a8893477c251bcee.ai_summary_short:
**Root Cause:** Four PTP boundary-clock clockClass tests failed in telco5g-ptp CI (bc, dualnicbc, dualnicbcha serial suites). Three failures are confirmed from test_results.json; all are clockClass-related timeouts (~379–385s, consistent with 5-minute Ginkgo Eventually windows). The "BC clock class recovers to Locked after upstream link outage" test (failed in bc and dualnicbc, and likely dualnicbcha) never enables gmCapable=1 on BC PtpConfigs. Test configs ship with gmCapable=0 (`test/pkg/testconfig/testconfig.go`), so ptp4l does not change clock class when upstream sync is lost—the metric stays at class 6 and the "degrade away from Locked (6)" assertion times out. Additionally, `PortEngine.TurnAllPortsDown`/`TurnAllPortsUp` (used by the outage test) only run `ip link set down/up` without disabling NetworkManager, unlike `TurnOffAndWaitFaulty` which was fixed in 6d2f1ddc. NM can auto-recover interfaces in 4–7s, preventing sustained outage. The bc-serial "Verify clockClass when locking PTP source on single NIC boundary clock" test already sets gmCapable and disables NM per-interface, but still timed out (~379s)—likely a timing/flake on OCP 4.17 node-level metrics or slow ptp4l reload; dual-NIC verify clockClass passed in the same run.

**Breaking PR/Commit:** da824bf0 ("Add T-BC clock class recovery test for upstream link outage", PR #185) introduced the outage recovery test without gmCapable setup. ccd1e711 ("Replace RecoverySlaveNetworkOutage with PortEngine", PR #222) refactored outage handling to TurnAllPortsDown/Up without carrying over the NM-management fix applied later in 6d2f1ddc (PR #215) to TurnOffAndWaitFaulty only.

**Affected Images:** OCP 4.17 nightly payload (4.17.0-0.nightly-2026-06-05-040541); PTP operator image built from release-4.17 branch (`image-registry.../openshift-ptp/ptp-operator:4.17`); bundled linuxptp-daemon and cloud-event-proxy sidecar from the telco5g CI pipeline (downstream OCP images, not upstream main in this job).

**Is it a flak

fb5b5817f5cf323c.root_cause: The `telco5g-ptp-tests` step never reached the Ginkgo e2e suite. After building and deploying `ptp-operator:4.20` onto the telco5g bare-metal cluster (`ptpcimno`, OCP 4.21.0), the CI script blocked indefinitely on `kubectl rollout status daemonset linuxptp-daemon -nopenshift-ptp`. The DaemonSet reac
fb5b5817f5cf323c.ai_summary_short:
**Root Cause:** The `telco5g-ptp-tests` step never reached the Ginkgo e2e suite. After building and deploying `ptp-operator:4.20` onto the telco5g bare-metal cluster (`ptpcimno`, OCP 4.21.0), the CI script blocked indefinitely on `kubectl rollout status daemonset linuxptp-daemon -nopenshift-ptp`. The DaemonSet reached 5 of 6 pods available, then regressed (3→2→1 updated pods), indicating a stuck/failing rolling update on at least one node. The step hit the Prow **3h45m timeout** (`process timed out`, exit 127). During the long hang, the cluster API became unreachable (`Unauthorized` / credential errors from ~07:45 UTC), which caused all gather/must-gather post-steps to fail as secondary symptoms. Cluster setup itself passed with all 6 nodes Ready.

**Breaking PR/Commit:** Not identifiable — no ptp-operator code regression found. The job passed on the previous run (2063848682771451904, 2026-06-08) and failed on this run (2064573497291575296, 2026-06-10). Recent ptp-operator commits are ART syncs and unrelated test/CI improvements. The failure is environmental, not introduced by a specific commit.

**Affected Images:**
- `image-registry.openshift-image-registry.svc:5000/openshift-ptp/ptp-operator:4.20` (built in-cluster during test)
- `quay.io/openshift/origin-ptp:4.20` (linuxptp-daemon, from release-4.20 operator env defaults — only the operator image was rebuilt; daemon/sidecar images were not)
- `quay.io/openshift/origin-cloud-event-proxy:4.20`
- `quay.io/podman/stable:v4.9.4` (in-cluster image build pod)

**Is it a flake?** yes — The same periodic job passed 2 days prior (~2h runtime). This run failed because the `linuxptp-daemon` DaemonSet rollout hung on the shared telco5g hardware cluster, likely due to a node-level issue (PTP-capable NIC missing/unavailable, pod crash loop, or transient cluster API instability on bare metal). The gather failures are downstream of the cluster entering an error/unreachable state.

**Issue Class:** infra_other

**Suggested Fix:**

e4e8d95e6f629b7f.root_cause: The `dualnicbcha` (Dual NIC Boundary Clock with phc2sys HA) serial test **"Slave can sync to master"** failed because the secondary BC slave interface `ens4f2` on ptpconfig `test-bc-master2` (node `cnfdf30.telco5gran.eng.rdu2.redhat.com`) never left **FAULTY** state and did not achieve **SLAVE/LOCKE
e4e8d95e6f629b7f.ai_summary_short:
**Root Cause:** The `dualnicbcha` (Dual NIC Boundary Clock with phc2sys HA) serial test **"Slave can sync to master"** failed because the secondary BC slave interface `ens4f2` on ptpconfig `test-bc-master2` (node `cnfdf30.telco5gran.eng.rdu2.redhat.com`) never left **FAULTY** state and did not achieve **SLAVE/LOCKED** sync within the 10-minute timeout. The primary BC interface `ens2f1` (`test-bc-master1`) synced successfully (offset within ±100 ns). After DualNICBCHA configs were deployed and `linuxptp-daemon` pods restarted (~15:55:05), PortEngine initialization at 15:55:52 already recorded `ens2f1: SLAVE` but `ens4f2: FAULTY`. The failure occurred at `ptptesthelper.go:93` via `BasicClockSyncCheck` → `CheckClockRoleAndOffset`.

**Breaking PR/Commit:** Not identifiable. The job deployed `release-5.0` downstream images built in-cluster. No recent commit in the available history clearly introduces a DualNICBCHA regression. The same interface (`ens4f2`) was **SLAVE** during the earlier `dualnicbc` mode in this same job run, pointing to a DualNICBCHA-specific sync or stabilization issue rather than a recent code regression.

**Affected Images:**
- `image-registry.openshift-image-registry.svc:5000/openshift-ptp/ptp-operator:5.0`
- `image-registry.openshift-image-registry.svc:5000/openshift-ptp/linuxptp-daemon:5.0`
- `image-registry.openshift-image-registry.svc:5000/openshift-ptp/cloud-event-proxy:5.0`

**Is it a flake?** **Uncertain, leaning no** — `ens4f2` was FAULTY from the start of the DualNICBCHA run and never recovered over 10+ minutes, which is more consistent with a real sync failure under the HA topology than a transient timing issue. However, only 1 of 6 test modes failed, and the same hardware passed `dualnicbc` minutes earlier, so lab state after the mode switch cannot be ruled out. Retry the job to confirm.

**Issue Class:** test_failure

**Suggested Fix:**
1. Inspect `linuxptp-daemon` logs from run `run_2026-06-07_15-54-12_serial_dualnicbcha` for `ens4f2` /

379e371690d1d4ed.root_cause: The `telco5g-ptp-tests` step failed because the shared telco5g bare-metal cluster API became unreachable during the initial `build_images()` phase. After `oc delete namespace openshift-ptp` succeeded, the test hung on a `kubectl wait` for namespace termination. The API server at `10.8.34.117:6443` t
379e371690d1d4ed.ai_summary_short:
**Root Cause:** The `telco5g-ptp-tests` step failed because the shared telco5g bare-metal cluster API became unreachable during the initial `build_images()` phase. After `oc delete namespace openshift-ptp` succeeded, the test hung on a `kubectl wait` for namespace termination. The API server at `10.8.34.117:6443` then began returning `i/o timeout` (~07:34 UTC), then `Unauthorized` (~07:45 UTC), and eventually `connection refused` (~08:11 UTC). The step retried for the full **3h45m** Prow timeout and exited with code **127**. No PTP conformance tests ran. Post-test gather steps also failed with `Unauthorized`, and cluster state showed `ClusterVersion: Installing ""` with missing cluster operators — indicating the cluster was dead or being torn down, not a PTP operator defect.

**Breaking PR/Commit:** Not identifiable in the ptp-operator repo. This was a **pj-rehearse** run of [openshift/release PR #80182](https://github.com/openshift/release/pull/80182) ("TEST ONLY - DO NOT MERGE"). That PR only changes CI wiring (`SKIP_OCP_INSTALL="true"` and cloning `yaronh12/ptp-operator` branch `ts2phc-gm-deletion-tests-coverage` instead of upstream). The failure occurred before image build, operator deploy, or conformance tests — so it is not caused by those PR changes or any ptp-operator commit.

**Affected Images:** Intended but never built/deployed due to early cluster failure:
- `image-registry.openshift-image-registry.svc:5000/openshift-ptp/ptp-operator:4.22`
- `image-registry.openshift-image-registry.svc:5000/openshift-ptp/linuxptp-daemon:4.22`
- `image-registry.openshift-image-registry.svc:5000/openshift-ptp/cloud-event-proxy:4.22`
- Release payload: `registry.ci.openshift.org/ocp/release:4.22.0-0.nightly-2026-06-09-152539`
- Test runner: `ci-telco-runner-latest`

**Is it a flake?** **Yes** — the telco5g cluster infrastructure became unavailable mid-test. All 6 nodes were Ready at test start (06:58 UTC); cluster-setup succeeded in 38s. The API degraded ~35 minutes into th
